scope_note: Classification and recommendation only. No promotion, rewrite, or canonicalization. No file moved into docs/architecture/ or docs/technical-specifications/. Nothing accepted.

classification_matrix.rationale: Latest corrected architecture package; supersedes the -draft package per its CORRECTION_REPORT.
20 technical specs incl. state machines (18) and failure/outcome taxonomy (19).
Machine-readable architecture/requirement/component/interface/traceability/decisions registries underpinning the specs.
Reviewer checklists + engineering learning guide; supporting, not canonical architecture.
Package self-validation reports (registry/ID/link/mermaid/completeness) + evidence index.

classification_matrix.recommended_destination: archive_only (retain as historical; do not promote)
classification_matrix.rationale: Superseded by the corrected package (CORRECTION_REPORT: '-draft package is preserved unchanged'). Duplicate-of corrected.
Native editable Draw.io for DIAG-01..22 + DIAG-S1..S3. Derived from the corrected package Mermaid + 18_ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAM_MANIFEST.md (see conflict C1).
Machine index of diagrams with registry-derived requirements/decisions/provenance.
Mermaid-CLI-rendered SVGs (one per diagram). Regenerable from source.
Offline static viewer generated from the diagram sources; not authoritative; regenerable.
Diagram package validation (semantic comparison, drawio editability, mermaid render, html viewer, manifest).
Accepted technical evidence baseline (reports 00-13 + index + raw-command-output) the corrected package is grounded on.
First discovery package; 'directionally useful but incomplete and materially wrong' per evidence-completion master report. Preliminary supporting only.
Explicitly rejected as an evidence source by the corrected package (01_SOURCE_AND_AUTHORITY_REGISTER). Retain as history only; conflict C3.
Sprint-level Antigravity validation/review packages (accepted-with-corrections in their sprints). Distinct from the rejected program-level package above.
Pre-execution approval packages for past sprints; decision history, not canonical architecture.

classification_matrix.recommended_destination: archive_only (cross-check against GOV-005 before any reuse)
classification_matrix.rationale: Capability-governance design; overlaps current GOV-005 governance — reuse only after comparison; conflict C4.
Hermes read-only audit + governed baseline records; evidence; Hermes remains inactive (0/6).
Git/branch model decision history.
Review templates / ChatGPT mentor prompt style; supporting, not architecture.
GOV-004 migration report + per-file source manifest; authoritative provenance for duplicates/generated/excluded files.
Archive readme.

duplicates.relationship: corrected SUPERSEDES draft; draft preserved as history
duplicates.recommendation: promote corrected only; keep draft archive-only

duplicates.primary: Mermaid blocks inside corrected package docs + 18_ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAM_MANIFEST.md
duplicates.relationship: diagrams are a second representation rendered/derived from the corrected-package Mermaid source
duplicates.recommendation: designate one canonical diagram source (see conflict C1); SVG/HTML are generated/regenerable

missing_inputs.impact: rendered SVGs are not reproducible from the repo alone; record the toolchain/version as a build dependency if SVGs are promoted

missing_inputs.status: HUMAN_REVIEW_PENDING per the diagram package README
missing_inputs.impact: the HTML viewer's interactive QA is not completed; do not represent as verified

missing_inputs.item: Source-Markdown sibling links in the diagram package
missing_inputs.detail: viewer 'Source Markdown' links work only when magna-enso-architecture-technical-specification-corrected sits beside the diagram package
missing_inputs.impact: if promoted to different canonical folders, these relative links break and must be re-pointed

conflicts.title: Canonical diagram source ambiguity
conflicts.detail: Two diagram representations exist: (a) Mermaid blocks + 18_ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAM_MANIFEST.md inside the corrected package, and (b) the .drawio sources in the diagrams package. Both claim to be editable source.

conflicts.title: corrected vs draft promotion
conflicts.detail: Both spec packages are archived; corrected supersedes draft. Confirm corrected is the sole promotion candidate and draft remains archive-only.

conflicts.title: Rejected program-level Antigravity validation present in archive
conflicts.detail: magna-program-antigravity-independent-validation is an explicitly rejected evidence source yet archived alongside accepted sprint-level Antigravity validations. Must not be promoted or cited.

conflicts.title: Historical capability-governance design vs current GOV-005
conflicts.detail: sprint-3-capability-governance-design overlaps current GOV-005 governance; reuse only after explicit comparison to avoid resurrecting superseded design.

conflicts.title: Nothing accepted
conflicts.detail: All architecture/spec/diagram materials are DRAFT_FOR_HUMAN_REVIEW; promotion to docs/architecture or docs/technical-specifications requires ARCH-001 execution + Product Owner approval (out of scope here).

docs/architecture/: corrected package 00..18 + supplemental reports (CORRECTION_REPORT, FINAL_CONSISTENCY_CORRECTION_REPORT, TRACE_SOURCE_RESOLUTION, PRESGN_TO_EVOLUTION_RELATIONSHIP_MATRIX, HERMES_DERIVED_CAPABILITY_PLAN, HELIX_VERSIONING_OPTIONS)
recommended_promotion_map.note: Recommendation only. Actual promotion/move is performed by ARCH-001 (not this task) after Product Owner approval.

decisions_required: C1: choose the canonical diagram source (Mermaid-in-docs+manifest vs .drawio).
C2: confirm corrected supersedes draft for promotion; draft stays archive-only.
C3: confirm the rejected program-level Antigravity package stays archive-only and is never cited.
C4: confirm whether sprint-3 capability-governance-design is reconciled with GOV-005 before any reuse.
C5: authorize (or defer) promotion to docs/architecture and docs/technical-specifications via ARCH-001; nothing is accepted by this task.
Record the Mermaid CLI version + viewer QA status as promotion prerequisites for rendered diagrams.
